name: Marielena Walz
affiliation: Equity Design (founder and CEO); NYC/NYS MWBE
proposals: Fix the slow city payment cycle so small businesses (and their subcontractors) are not paid a year after the work; close the cash-flow gap that prevents taking on large contracts
Raise or allow upward revision of the $25,000 MWBE contract cap with DOE for proven, established vendors, with an evidence-based process to increase it
quote: we get paid after the fact, sometimes a year after the fact ... So, I have to figure out how to be able to close that gap in my revenue so I have the cash flow that would allow me to take on big contracts.

name: Anedi Paneiagua
affiliation: Independent family child care provider
proposals: Include independent / family-environment child care providers (not just those in networks) in any expansion of child care access, funding and growth opportunities
Preserve parents' freedom to choose the program (center, school, or family-based) that fits their child rather than judging by network affiliation
Use existing licensed provider capacity to reduce the 25,000+ child waiting list and simplify access for families; unify the fragmented system (subsidized, Head Start, preK, 3K)
quote: any kind of expansion of the child care can be included for the providers, independent providers ... and can guarantee that no child, family or educator can be left behind.

name: Dr. Constance Barnes
affiliation: Unmask EU LLC (founder); chair, economic development committee, Community Board 8 (testifying as a community member)
proposals: Streamline the MWBE application paperwork (e.g., accommodate applicants whose landlords don't provide rent receipts)
Consider a vacancy tax on landlords holding empty commercial storefronts so those spaces can be used by small businesses

Greatly expand SBS Business Express on-site support teams (only ~a dozen citywide) and do more grassroots, in-person outreach to businesses that lack technology or awareness rather than requiring online/cross-borough travel
quote: maybe a vacancy tax for landlords who are just holding businesses that could be utilized. We should also provide on-site community support.

name: Patrick Hall
affiliation: Alain Flowers, Tribeca (co-owner with wife Christine); storefront business since 1983
proposals: Require a small business economic impact statement for every piece of legislation, regulation or major policy affecting businesses, measuring the cost to a privately owned, owner-operated small business in time, paperwork, compliance, risk and money
'Listen before legislating' — bring actual small business owners to the table before any new requirement, mandate or program is created
Stop treating small businesses as a single category; recognize they are a diverse ecosystem (new vs. legacy, restaurant vs. flower shop, sole proprietor vs. 20 employees)
quote: Listen before legislating. Before a new requirement is added, before a mandate is created, before a program is launched, bring in the people who will actually have to live with it.

name: Daniel Garcia
affiliation: Sasa Catering and Special Events (owner); MWBE; Port Morris Industrial Business Zone, Bronx
proposals: Fix slow city-agency payments that cause cash-flow crises for industrial businesses (his receivables hit $2M; needed $750K in bridge financing)
Reverse the planned defunding of the Industrial Business Service Provider (IBSP) program that funds the BACH industrial team, which is a 'lifeline' for industrial businesses
quote: When payments are delayed for months or even years, it creates a real cash flow crisis that can threaten whole operations like salsa.

name: Andrew Rigie
affiliation: NYC Hospitality Alliance (executive director)
proposals: Remove the revocable consent requirement from the outdoor dining (Dining Out NYC) licensing process via charter change — it adds 3+ months and is the major reason licensing now takes 6+ months
Consolidate the two separate licensing processes (sidewalk cafe vs. roadway cafe) which have different standards/timeframes
Reduce or eliminate the consent fees (per-square-footage) that act as barriers while keeping proper community and agency review
quote: removing the revocable consent requirement from the outdoor dining licensing process ... by removing the revocable consent you will speed up the process by months and months and months.

name: Michelle Maniah
affiliation: Family child care provider (5 years)
proposals: Make 2K/3K inclusive so traditional/independent providers are not forced to join networks (which strips their independence and adds layered agency oversight)
Restore child care access/vouchers (ACS, HRA) for children not in networks and for children under age two; address the ~60,000 families being dropped from ACS vouchers
Fund after-school care for 3- and 4-year-olds (gap from 2pm-6pm) and provide capital/financing so home-based providers can expand to center-based programs; expand CDA-credential instructor capacity
quote: the 2K and 3K program forces you to join a network ... there is nothing being done about these providers who are going to fall behind and eventually have to close their doors.

name: Jessica Hower
affiliation: Political consultant; advisory board, Go Vote NYC; former executive director, New Majority NYC
proposals: Adopt open primaries in New York City to roughly double turnout and make elections more efficient (80% of US states already have them)
Frame open primaries as enfranchising independent voters — nearly half of NYC's independents are under 40 and a majority are people of color — currently excluded by the closed primary system
quote: Open primary districts generally have twice the turnout that we do here. And since this is also an efficiency discussion, election processes are much more efficient.

name: Yadira Rodriguez
affiliation: Construction consultant, Bronx business owner
proposals: Reduce the financial burden of permitting fees, violation penalties and lengthy approval processes from agencies like DOB and OATH
Lower agency fees that burden homeowners (who can't afford architects/engineers/GCs), which in turn hurts contractors' ability to run a functional business in the Bronx
quote: the financial burden created by the permitting fees, violation penalties, and the lengthy approval process by the numerous agencies such as Do and Oath ... their fees are incredible crazy.

name: Josea Kanan
affiliation: Balance Money and Mind (founder/owner); financial literacy, bookkeeping, tax; NYC MWBE
proposals: Address the MWBE procurement disparity (per the FY25 report, ~78% of MWBE firms got no contracts; MWBE average contract ~$754K vs ~$3.6M for non-certified firms)
Require prime contractors who win city contracts to hire/subcontract small and micro businesses from the Bronx so NYC procurement dollars stay with local New Yorkers
quote: whoever earned those contract can be secured that they hire or should contract a small and micro businesses from the Bronx to ensure that those resource from New Yorkers always stay in New York.

name: Madison Weisenberger
name_as_heard: Madison E. Weisenberger (stage name Madison Ford)
affiliation: Actress, director, content creator; NYC resident (since Dec.)
proposals: Designate engaging in (or knowingly associating with / financing / housing) sex trafficking as an act of terrorism, including banks, hotels and businesses that profit from it

Have NYC collaborate with international police (e.g., Interpol) and NYPD/state police to share sex-trafficking data, and create a visible public database of those associated with trafficking; build a culture of reporting
quote: engaging in sex trafficking or associating with a person that you're aware is presently engaging in sex trafficking should be designated as an act of terrorism.

name: Charles Delhomme
affiliation: BACH Network (Industrial Business Service Provider for the Bronx)
proposals: Streamline permitting across agencies, strengthen protections for IBZ-zoned land, improve infrastructure responsiveness in industrial corridors, and give industrial businesses a real seat at economic-development planning
Reverse the planned citywide defunding of the Industrial Business Service Provider (IBSP) program (nearly 40 years old) that connects industrial businesses to city government
quote: The industrial business service providing program ... is now scheduled to be defunded citywide ... Cutting this program would eliminate one of the few consistent voices for the industrial sector.

name: Amalu Saju Jial
affiliation: PH High-Tech (founder/CEO), a technology consulting company; platform MineLog X AI
proposals: Create more pathways to funding, public partnerships and pilot/test-and-deploy opportunities for minority-owned and immigrant businesses
Use open-source AI data platforms to make NYC construction, infrastructure, transportation and public sectors safer and more cost-effective (predicting failures before they happen)
quote: New York has an incredible opportunity to unlock the potential of immigrants and minority entrepreneurs by creating more pathways to funding, public partnerships and opportunities to test and deploy innovative solutions locally.

name: Pedro Suarez
affiliation: Third Avenue Business Improvement District, South Bronx (executive director)
proposals: Enforce fair-share distribution of shelters/substance-abuse facilities (cites 2023 Comptroller report; Bronx is oversaturated) with real accountability
Build a single app/web account consolidating all city permitting and licensing for small businesses, multilingual
Make DOT responsible for proactively auditing/fixing street lighting (lights out for months) rather than relying on business owners to report; invest more in public-realm programming/plazas
Create a small business emergency fund for natural disasters/fires/floods/financial hardship; lower insurance and energy costs (work with Con Ed)

Streamline city MWBE contracting/reimbursement into one transparent system (registration can take 6 months to a year); add commercial-lease protections for legacy businesses; integrate entrepreneurship into high school and higher education
quote: there should be an app or web account to consolidate all of the city's permitting and licensing for small businesses ... in multiple languages is some low-hanging fruit.

name: Gregory Hernandez
affiliation: Bronx business owner; filmmaker; production company (founded 2019); MBE certified, DOE vendor
proposals: Regulate AI in the contracting/bidding process — train procurement officers to verify that bid content (e.g., drone/aerial footage) was actually created by the bidding business, not AI-generated or masqueraded, to level the playing field

Provide a payroll cash-infusion/financing mechanism for businesses in good standing (e.g., three years of tax returns) so solo entrepreneurs can hire full-time local Bronx graduates / CTE completers rather than waiting up to a year to get paid on contracts
quote: can we regulate AI in the sense of ... make sure that content that is created during the bidding process is actually created by that particular individual or that business entity and not created by AI.

name: Jerry Hippolyte
affiliation: Housing Solutions of New York (chief of staff and head of government affairs); Bronx-based homelessness nonprofit
proposals: Strengthen coordination across city agencies ('one government') with shared/integrated reporting platforms so nonprofits don't submit the same information repeatedly to DOB, FDNY, DOT, etc.
Modernize procurement and payment processes — build further on the Passport system so its 'vault' documents (e.g., a COI) connect across multiple contracts instead of being re-uploaded each time
Reduce duplication of submissions across systems so organizations can spend time on service delivery instead of bureaucracy
quote: there's a vault section but that vault section doesn't connect back to your contract. So if you have multicontracts you still have to go to the vault versus ... it can cut across the other contracts.

name: Belis Laura
name_as_heard: Bel Belle kiss Laura / Belis Laura
affiliation: Grand Slam (founder/CEO), a family-owned banquet hall business (18 years); also has a youth foundation
proposals: Enforce business loading zones so other drivers don't block them, and stop ticketing the business's own commercial vehicles while they are actively loading/unloading (treat it as the city's responsibility to clear illegally parked cars, not the business owner's)
Improve street and park cleanliness in the community
quote: They give us ticket every two minutes, every five minutes ... it's becoming like another rent to us.

name: Sarah Wilson
affiliation: Former foster parent (not a small business owner)
proposals: Create a legal mechanism guaranteeing continued visitation with the foster family when a child enters care young (e.g., before age two) and stays more than two years, rather than pushing reunification 'at all cost'

Reframe reunification as an 'aspiration' rather than a 'goal,' and commission research into how many children re-enter foster care after failed reunifications (to judge whether reunification is actually successful)
quote: this push for reunification at all cost that is often harming children ... there should be some kind of legal mechanism ... to ensure visitation with that foster family.

name: Marcus Khan
affiliation: Bronx resident; student, Colin Powell School for Civic and Global Leadership, City College (CUNY)
proposals: Shift city budgeting/spending toward long-term preventive measures (housing and food security) rather than short-term reactive ones, to address root causes of gang violence, homelessness and poor outcomes
Support community land trusts (e.g., Port Morris Community Land Trust), more supermarkets to end food deserts, after-school programs, and legislation like COPA / safer-homes measures
quote: we need to stop the fall before it happens or else we can't be surprised when we're in the hospital.

name: Mimi Turner
affiliation: Women's Housing and Economic Development Corporation (WHEDco), senior small business adviser; South Bronx community development nonprofit
proposals: Sustain and expand city investment in community-based local development organizations (like WHEDco) that help small businesses navigate licenses, leases and the new gate regulation
Prioritize cooperative ownership models (e.g., succession planning, the Bronx People's Federal Credit Union) as an economic development strategy
Evaluate food access initiatives including the FRESH program so future investment goes beyond attracting operators to ensure affordability, quality jobs, local procurement and local ownership
quote: we urge the city to sustain and expand investment in community-based local development focused organizations like Wedco.

name: Lamar White
affiliation: Bronx resident; medical coder, real estate agent, software developer; founder of the Quality Time Network and Quality Time Network Foundation
proposals: Create a clear pathway for residents, community organizers and local innovators who have already built civic-tech solutions to connect with the right city stakeholders and run pilot programs that could inform policy
Use the charter review process to establish clearer mechanisms for such solutions to be evaluated, tested and implemented across NYC
quote: What is the best path to connect with the right city stakeholders and explore pilot programs that could eventually inform policy and improve how services are delivered?

name: Janessa Jules
affiliation: South Bronx small business owner (cannabis space) and analyst
proposals: Conduct a low-cost audit of all NYC government/agency websites to fix redundant sites, dead links, wrong contact info and out-of-service numbers — reducing strain on 311 and saving staff hours
Create an exemption from the mandatory distributor/delivery-entity requirement for small/startup cannabis businesses (which adds cost for low-volume sellers)
Improve communication/marketing about online voter registration availability ('why should you care about voting') rather than assuming people don't want to register
quote: doing an audit of all of the different agencies and department sites could be something that would be a lowcost way to address that issue.

name: Mohamad Salu Diello
name_as_heard: Mamadu Sa Salu Dio / Mohamad Salu Diello
affiliation: President, Guinean community in New York (nonprofit advocating for Guineans in NY)
proposals: Improve access to funding/investment for immigrant-owned small businesses (restaurants, general stores) that are struggling with rising prices
Give immigrant restaurant owners equal opportunity to win contracts to deliver culturally familiar food to shelters (raised during the recent migrant influx)
quote: the access to funding I might say is very limited to us. I really ... asking you to consider that aspect.

name: B. Saj
affiliation: Broker/owner of Bar Real Estate and owner of City Builder Development Corporation (buy/fix/flip)
proposals: Require DOB plan examiners to give all objections at once instead of serial round-trips, since each delay (while paying ~12% hard-money interest on a 6-month loan) eats the small developer's profit
Speed up HPD inspections to clear violations (e.g., heat/hot water) — currently takes three to four months after a property is fully renovated
quote: Why don't you tell me what you need ... one time when I submit it to you, you don't have to make me go back and forth.

name: Ana Wahed
affiliation: Tara Kitchen (founder), boutique Moroccan restaurant group; certified NYC MWBE
proposals: Make micro-purchasing (city purchases below $25,000) transparent and accessible to small businesses like independent restaurants (food/boxed lunches/breakfasts) — currently convoluted with no success after 18 months of trying
Overhaul the outdated MWBE/procurement websites (Passport, etc.) and modernize the certification/upload/payment systems
quote: I'm really interested in micropurchasing and if that is something that could be made transparent and ... accessible to small businesses like myself. It's really the difference between us surviving and going out of business.

name: Dearis Zumani
affiliation: Founder of Neighbor First Group (helps small businesses get funding)
proposals: Have the city help small businesses obtain SBA loans more easily, given that roughly half of applications are declined and approval rates are very low against high demand
Provide affordable financing for startup businesses that lack sufficient income, since conventional bank loans (20-25% interest) are unaffordable
quote: the best funding for me is the SBA loan but it's very hard to get.

name: Madu (Mamadou)
affiliation: Deputy director, Guinea USA Chamber of Commerce (nonprofit; not a business owner)
proposals: Posed questions/asks (rather than proposals): what specific programs help immigrant-owned small businesses grow; how can they access city grants/low-interest loans/financial assistance
Provide support for entrepreneurs who face language barriers navigating government services, and programs helping small businesses obtain city contracts/procurement opportunities
quote: what support is available for entrepreneur who face language [barriers] when navigate government services?

name: Glenny Payano
affiliation: CEO of a domestic violence nonprofit and an LLC (primarily Spanish-speaking)
proposals: Create programs/licensing pathways so moms can legally run a business from home (or affordable local space) without getting ticketed, given high commercial rent and many empty storefronts

Fix the HRA/social-assistance self-service application system (broken scanners, document problems, English-only) that wrongly rejects non-English-speaking applicants; provide real cultural-competence and language-access help, plus programs for low-income mothers who lack credit history
quote: It is an abuse ... for them to assume that they can use the system and fill out applications without any kind of help and do it by themselves in another language.

name: Mariam Diallo
affiliation: Owner of Guinea African Hair Braiding (two salons); secretary/director of Guinea US Chamber of Commerce
proposals: Create a separate licensing pathway (or exemption) for natural hair braiding, which uses no chemicals — removing the costly NY State requirement (14 weeks of school plus state exam) that is an unnecessary barrier driving women to braid illegally or move to other states
Improve access to affordable financing/grants and address rising commercial rent, utility and insurance costs for women- and immigrant-owned businesses
quote: in many states, they don't require a license to braid a hair because when we braid hair, we don't use no chemical product ... that's a very big barrier for many talented women who want to work.

name: Pascal (AK General Contractor)
affiliation: Licensed general contractor (AK General Contractor)
proposals: Lower the high cost/barriers of getting a contractor license and insurance, which pushes many to work unlicensed

Address the inability of small contractors to park commercial trucks on the street at night (ticketed, can't afford a parking lot/office) and the new building-department requirement to hire a superintendent on projects, which small businesses can't afford
quote: the building department changed the law if you got like some project you have to hiding ... superintendent that's the extra money we have to pay and we don't have enough budget.

name: Sarah Espayat
affiliation: MWBE-certified business owner; mental health / emotional coaching services for women ages 25-55
proposals: Create a way for prime contractors / large organizations to recognize and use small MWBE service providers as subcontractors, since they currently overlook or don't feel confident in small community-rooted providers when they win contracts
quote: they don't consider us like ... how can we put something together that let them see us and consider us when they get these type of contracts.

name: Jessica Lopez
affiliation: Licensed real estate agent (Bronx); 20+ years in property management; works with voucher holders (CityFHEPS/FHEPS)
proposals: Speed up rental-assistance voucher processing (CityFHEPS/FHEPS) so it actually meets the 30-45 day target instead of 3-4 months, so landlords don't give up holding vacant units and voucher holders don't lose apartments

Recognize and protect real estate agents as small business owners/independent contractors; don't cut broker compensation while delays and responsibilities grow, or families lose the people who help them navigate the system
quote: having a voucher does not mean they have a home ... I've personally had families wait 3 to four months after submitting everything before receiving final approval to move in.

name: Marilyn/Meline Suarez
affiliation: Founder of a Region NYC small business making artisanal paletas (multicultural flavors); immigrant entrepreneur
proposals: Provide additional support for the food inspection/Article 20-C licensing process — educational workshops, clear checklists, multilingual resources and easier technical assistance — especially for first-time owners
Fix the confusing NY State Department of Agriculture website/PDF forms (no translation, 20-page PDFs) that make compliance hard
quote: it can be difficult to know exactly what is expected ... Additional support such as educational workshops, clear checklists, multilingual resources, and easier technical assistant will make a significant difference.
